title: Book Club App
tagline: A full-stack book tracking platform built from scratch using Node.js, Express, EJS, and SQLite without relying on frontend frameworks.

features.title: User Authentication
features.desc: Secure user registration, login, logout, password hashing, and session-based authentication with protected routes.

features.title: Book Management
features.desc: Create, browse, edit, and delete books while ensuring users can only modify their own submissions.

features.title: Reading Tracker
features.desc: Track reading status, monitor page progress, and organize books into Want to Read, Reading, and Finished collections.

features.title: Ratings & Reviews
features.desc: Write, edit, and delete book reviews with star ratings while displaying community averages for every book.

features.title: Search & Discovery
features.desc: Quickly locate books by title, author, or genre using integrated search and filtering capabilities.

features.title: Robust Backend Validation
features.desc: Performs comprehensive server-side validation, graceful error handling, custom error pages, and user-friendly feedback.

context: Book Club App is a personal learning project built to explore full-stack web development without relying on frontend frameworks. The goal was to understand how modern web applications work under the hood by implementing routing, authentication, server-side rendering, relational databases, sessions, validation, and CRUD operations using only Express, EJS, and SQLite. Throughout the project, emphasis was placed on writing modular code, designing a maintainable architecture, and creating an intuitive user experience while keeping the technology stack intentionally lightweight.

tech.name: Node.js
tech.role: Runs the backend server and application logic.

tech.name: Express.js
tech.role: Handles routing, middleware, authentication, and HTTP request processing.

tech.name: EJS
tech.role: Provides server-side rendering for dynamic HTML pages.

tech.name: SQLite
tech.role: Stores users, books, reading progress, and reviews in a lightweight relational database.

tech.name: Express Session
tech.role: Maintains authenticated user sessions across requests.

tech.name: CSS
tech.role: Creates a responsive and consistent user interface without CSS frameworks.
